result:
Git write operations are hitting your session's approval gate for every command. Since `git status` (read) works but `git add`/`git switch` (write) don't, here are the three commands to paste and run — they'll complete the PR in one go:

```bash
git switch -c claude/daily-report-2026-06-27 \
  && git add reports/2026-06-27-daily-summary.md \
  && git commit -m "chore(report): daily summary 2026-06-27 — cred-health emitter + plaid portability fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>" \
  && git push -u origin claude/daily-report-2026-06-27 \
  && gh pr create \
       --draft \
       --title "chore(report): daily summary 2026-06-27" \
       --body "$(cat reports/2026-06-27-daily-summary.md)"
```

---

Here's what the report covers (already written to `reports/2026-06-27-daily-summary.md`):

**One commit in the last 24h** (`98baea5`, 2026-06-26 22:46 PDT):

1. **`skills/datadog/cred-health.mjs`** — new script that reads the `createTime` of the latest Secret Manager version for each rotating OAuth token (Xero x4, QuickBooks) and emits `otc.fleet.token_age_hours` as a Datadog gauge. Wired into the token-keeper daily cron. Fail-open per secret. This gives an early-warning Datadog alert path before a token hits its idle-expiry cliff (Xero 60-day, QB 100-day) — prevents a repeat of the Stripe MCP "invalid refresh token" incident.

2. **`skills/plaid-banking/plaid.mjs`** — portability fix: when `PLAID_CLIENT_ID`/`PLAID_SECRET` env vars are absent (HyperAgent session), the script now self-hydrates them from GCP Secret Manager via the claude-driver SA JWT. Same pattern already used in `skills/xero/xero.mjs`. Makes the Plaid skill work identically on both Claude Code and HyperAgent.
